page: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, with one thread per core, supported by 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Large block read-only and write memory bandwidth tests both place in the top 25% tier, reaching 249.58 GB/sec and 105.6 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance is also notable, with Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU physics tests scoring in the top 25%. However, single-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, fall within the middle 50% average. Large-model LLM inference shows a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing for a 70B model falling into the bottom 25% tier, while text generation for a 7B model remains in the top 25%.

The dedicated physical core allocation and high-bandwidth network interface offer resource density suitable for high-performance computing workloads. The qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the ARM64 architecture, which provides competitive memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling relative to its resource footprint. This server is appropriate for workloads such as distributed scientific simulations, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processors running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data shows top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. Multi-core processing is also strong, ranking in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core and PassMark physics tests, though single-core performance and large-model LLM prompt processing represent relative tradeoffs. This server is designed for high-performance computing, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and scientific simulations.

128.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier memory bandwidth benchmarks.
High-speed baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.
Strong multi-core performance and database operation capabilities.
Appropriate for distributed scientific simulations, database tasks, and web serving.
